translation_en: The soul is never born and never dies.
As a person casts off worn-out clothes and puts on new ones, so the soul discards worn bodies and takes on new ones.

original_transliteration: karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana
translation_en: You have a right to action alone, never to its fruits.
Established in yoga, perform your actions.

original_transliteration: tasmād asaktaḥ satataṁ kāryaṁ karma samācara
translation_en: Therefore, always perform your duty, without attachment.

original_transliteration: yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati
translation_en: Whenever dharma declines and adharma rises, I manifest Myself.

original_transliteration: ye yathā māṁ prapadyante tāṁs tathaiva bhajāmy aham
translation_en: As people approach Me, so do I reciprocate.
Let a person lift himself by himself; the self is the friend and the enemy of the self.

original_transliteration: ananyāś cintayanto māṁ… yoga-kṣemaṁ vahāmy aham
translation_en: Those who think of Me alone, I carry what they lack and preserve what they have.

original_transliteration: adveṣṭā sarva-bhūtānāṁ maitraḥ karuṇa eva ca
translation_en: One who hates no being, who is friendly and compassionate, is dear to Me.

original_transliteration: sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja
translation_en: Abandon all duties and take refuge in Me alone.
Thou art That.
This Self is Brahman.
Not this, not this.
Truth alone triumphs.
One realizes that Brahman is bliss.
All this is pervaded by the Lord.

original_transliteration: śreyas ca preyas ca manuṣyam etas
translation_en: The good and the pleasant approach the person; the wise choose the good.
I am Brahman.

original_transliteration: nāsad āsīn no sad āsīt tadānīm
translation_en: Then, there was neither non-existence nor existence.
Victory to Hanuman, ocean of wisdom and virtue.
Knowing my body to be dull of intellect, I remember you, Hanuman.
Dharma protects those who protect it.
Bhakti is supreme love for God.
The lane of love is very narrow; two cannot pass through together.

original_transliteration: śrī rām jai rām jai jai rām
translation_en: Glory to Rama, victory to Rama.
We worship the three-eyed One, fragrant and nourishing all.

original_transliteration: ik oṅkār sat nām kartā purakh nirbhau nirvair
translation_en: There is One Reality; His Name is Truth, the Creator, without fear, without hate.
Wondrous is the Enlightener.

original_transliteration: hukamī andar sabh ko, bāhar hukam na ko-e
translation_en: All exist within His command; nothing is outside of it.
Conquer the mind and you conquer the world.
Through the Name, the spirit rises ever higher.
Mind precedes all phenomena.
Avoid evil, cultivate good, purify the mind — this is the teaching of the Buddhas.
All conditioned things are impermanent.
All phenomena are not-self.
May all beings be happy and safe.
Hail the Jewel in the Lotus.

original_transliteration: gate gate pāragate pārasaṁgate bodhi svāhā
translation_en: Gone, gone, gone beyond, utterly beyond — enlightenment, hail!
Non-violence is the highest dharma.
Souls support one another.
I bow to the Arihants, the enlightened ones.
The master, after the disciple eats, simply says: "Go wash your bowl."
"No thing"; a negation that points beyond yes/no.

original_transliteration: Before enlightenment, chop wood, carry water. After enlightenment, chop wood, carry water.
translation_en: Before enlightenment, chop wood, carry water. After enlightenment, chop wood, carry water.

original_transliteration: Mountains are mountains, waters are waters.
translation_en: Things are simply as they are — suchness.
Contacts of the senses with their objects give rise to cold and heat, pleasure and pain; they come and go, bear them patiently.
When one contemplates sense objects, attachment to them arises; from attachment springs desire, from desire anger is born.

original_transliteration: na hi jñānena sadṛśaṁ pavitram iha vidyate
translation_en: There is nothing in this world as purifying as knowledge.
The wise see equally a learned sage, a cow, an elephant, a dog, and an outcaste.
The mind is restless and hard to control, but it can be trained by practice and detachment.

original_transliteration: patraṁ puṣpaṁ phalaṁ toyaṁ yo me bhaktyā prayacchati
translation_en: Whoever offers Me a leaf, a flower, a fruit, or water with devotion, I accept that offering of love.
Therefore arise and win glory; conquer your foes and enjoy a flourishing kingdom.
That knowledge by which one sees a single imperishable reality in all beings is sattvic knowledge.

original_transliteration: na tatra cakṣur gacchati na vāg gacchati no manaḥ
translation_en: The eye does not reach there, nor speech, nor the mind; we do not know how to teach it.
The knot of the heart is broken, all doubts are cut, when the Supreme is seen.

original_transliteration: asato mā sad gamaya, tamaso mā jyotir gamaya, mṛtyor mā amṛtaṁ gamaya
translation_en: Lead me from the unreal to the real, from darkness to light, from death to immortality.

original_transliteration: sad eva saumya idam agra āsīd ekam evādvitīyam
translation_en: In the beginning, dear one, there was Being alone, one without a second.

original_transliteration: yas tu sarvāṇi bhūtāny ātmany evānupaśyati
translation_en: One who sees all beings in the Self and the Self in all beings never turns away.
Yoga is the stilling of the fluctuations of the mind.
The mind is controlled through practice and non-attachment.

original_transliteration: maitrī karuṇā muditopekṣāṇāṁ sukha-duḥkha-puṇyāpuṇya-viṣayāṇāṁ bhāvanātaś citta-prasādanam
translation_en: Through cultivation of friendliness, compassion, joy, and equanimity, the mind becomes serene.
Posture should be steady and comfortable.

original_transliteration: āhiṁsā satyam akrodhaḥ dānam śāntir apaiśunam
translation_en: Non-violence, truthfulness, freedom from anger, charity, tranquility, and freedom from malice — these constitute dharma.
The essence of all dharma is this: do not do unto others what causes pain if done to you.
Rama is dharma in living form.

original_transliteration: sochai soch na hova-ī je sochī lakh vār
translation_en: By thinking, the truth cannot be found, even if one thinks a hundred thousand times.
Sing and listen, and let your mind be filled with love.
Listening to the Name, sorrow and sin are destroyed.

original_transliteration: nānak nāvai bājh sanāth sabh koā
translation_en: Without the Name, all are desolate and false.
Speaking the Name I live; forgetting it, I die.
The Psalm of Peace: the Name of God is sweet ambrosial nectar.
Meditate, meditate in remembrance, and find peace.
May there be well-being for all.

original_transliteration: anand bha-i-ā merī mā-e satgurū mai pā-i-ā
translation_en: I am in bliss, O my mother, for I have found the True Guru.

original_transliteration: so dar kehā so ghar kehā jit bahi sarab samālhe
translation_en: Where is that door, where is that house in which You sit and take care of all?

original_transliteration: sach kahau sun le-hu sabhai jin prem kī-o tin hī prabh pā-i-o
translation_en: I speak the truth, listen all: only those who love attain the Lord.
source: Guru Granth Sahib, Guru Gobind Singh

original_transliteration: jab lag khalsa rahe niārā tab lag tej dīau mai sārā
translation_en: So long as the Khalsa remains distinct, I shall give them my full radiance.

original_transliteration: mānas kī jāt sabai ekai pahchānbo
translation_en: Recognize all humankind as one.

original_transliteration: thāl vich tinn vastū pa-ī-o sat santoakh vīchāro
translation_en: Upon this plate, three things are placed: truth, contentment, and contemplation.
source: Guru Granth Sahib, Guru Arjan Dev

original_transliteration: jo nar dukh meh dukh nahī mānai sukh sneh ar bhay nahīn jā kai
translation_en: One who does not feel pain in sorrow, who is unaffected by pleasure, attachment, and fear — know that person to be wise.
source: Guru Granth Sahib, Guru Tegh Bahadur

original_transliteration: bhay kahū ko det nahi nahi bhay mānat ān
translation_en: One who frightens no one and is not afraid of anyone — such a person is truly wise.
source: Guru Granth Sahib, Guru Tegh Bahadur

original_transliteration: vich dunīā sev kamā-ī-ai tā dargeh baiṣan pā-ī-ai
translation_en: In this world, engage in selfless service, and you shall find a seat in the Court of the Lord.

original_transliteration: ghāl khā-e kichh hathahu de-e nānak rāh pachāṇeh se-ī
translation_en: One who works honestly and shares with others knows the true path.

original_transliteration: miṭhatu nīvīn nānakā guṇ changāīāṁ tatu
translation_en: Sweetness and humility, O Nanak, are the essence of all virtues.

original_transliteration: gurmukh sukh phal piram ras chākhai
translation_en: The one oriented toward the Guru tastes the fruit of bliss and divine love.

original_transliteration: nām japai nām āradhe nām bole-ī ban āī
translation_en: Chant the Name, worship the Name; speaking the Name is the true calling.

original_transliteration: na hi verena verāni sammantīdha kudācanaṁ
translation_en: Hatred is never appeased by hatred in this world; by non-hatred alone is it appeased.
Heedfulness is the path to the deathless; heedlessness is the path to death.

original_transliteration: na paresaṁ vilomāni na paresaṁ katākataṁ
translation_en: Do not look at the faults of others but at your own deeds done and left undone.

original_transliteration: yo sahassaṁ sahassena saṅgāme mānuse jine ekañ ca jeyya-m-attānaṁ
translation_en: One who conquers himself is greater than one who conquers a thousand men in battle.

original_transliteration: attā hi attano nātho ko hi nātho paro siyā
translation_en: One is one's own refuge; what other refuge could there be?

original_transliteration: akkodhena jine kodhaṁ asādhuṁ sādhunā jine
translation_en: Conquer anger by non-anger; conquer wickedness by goodness.
You yourselves must strive; the Buddhas only point the way.
Form is emptiness; emptiness is form.
The wonderful dharma of the Lotus Flower: all beings possess the potential for Buddhahood.

original_transliteration: mātar yathā niyaṁ puttaṁ āyusā ekaputtaṁ anurakkhe
translation_en: As a mother would protect her only child with her life, so cultivate boundless love for all beings.
Let one walk alone like a rhinoceros horn, doing no harm to any living being.

original_transliteration: na sīlabbatena suddhiṁ āha na bahussaccena na samādhinā
translation_en: Purity comes not merely from rituals, learning, or concentration alone, but from letting go of clinging.

original_transliteration: mā anussavena mā paramparāya mā itikirāya
translation_en: Do not accept anything merely by tradition, hearsay, or authority; know for yourself what leads to suffering and what leads to wellbeing.
Be a lamp unto yourselves; take refuge in yourselves and in no other.
This is the noble truth of suffering: birth is suffering, aging is suffering, illness is suffering.
The middle way avoids both extremes and gives rise to calm, to insight, to awakening.
Compassion is the root of the bodhisattva's practice.

original_transliteration: buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gacchāmi, dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gacchāmi, saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gacchāmi
translation_en: I take refuge in the Buddha, I take refuge in the Dharma, I take refuge in the Sangha.
Right faith, right knowledge, and right conduct together constitute the path to liberation.
Attachment causes bondage of karma to the soul.
Karmic matter is shed through austerity and self-discipline.

original_transliteration: savvesim jīva-nikāyesim na hantavvā na ajjhāvayitavvā
translation_en: No living beings should be harmed, nor should one exercise power over them.

original_transliteration: khamemi savva-jīve savve jīvā khamantu me
translation_en: I forgive all beings; may all beings forgive me.
Forgiveness is the ornament of the brave.

original_transliteration: tumam si ṇāma sacceva jamhā meāvakamkhasī
translation_en: You are that which you wish to harm; you are that which you wish to dominate. In hurting others, you hurt yourself.

original_transliteration: soyaṁ bāle appaṇā appaṇaṁ kuvvai dukkhiyaṁ
translation_en: The ignorant one makes oneself suffer; one who understands this finds peace.
The soul itself is the supreme soul; the individual self is the highest reality.
Compassion is the root of all dharma.

original_transliteration: koha-m-koheṇa jei ṇam jaṁ jiyai, taṁ jaṁ dujjiyaṁ
translation_en: Anger conquered by anger becomes harder to conquer; anger conquered by calm is truly conquered.
Non-possessiveness is the supreme vow; attach yourself to nothing.

original_transliteration: appā kattā vikattā ya duhāṇa ya suhāṇa ya
translation_en: The self is the doer and un-doer of both suffering and happiness.

original_transliteration: ṇicchaya-ṇayeṇa ṇāṇī du appaṁ jāṇai kevalaṁ
translation_en: From the ultimate standpoint, the wise one knows only the self in its pure form.
Practice small vows daily: even a small step toward non-violence transforms the world.
Reality is many-sided; no single viewpoint captures the whole truth.

original_transliteration: namō arihantāṇaṁ, namō siddhāṇaṁ, namō āyariyāṇaṁ, namō uvajjhāyāṇaṁ, namō lōe savva-sāhūṇaṁ
translation_en: I bow to the enlightened, the liberated, the teachers, the preceptors, and all seekers in the world.

original_transliteration: bù shì fēng dòng, bù shì fān dòng, rén zhě xīn dòng
translation_en: It is not the flag that moves, nor the wind; it is only the mind that moves.

original_transliteration: hé qí zì xìng běn zì qīng jìng
translation_en: How wonderful that one's own nature is originally pure.

original_transliteration: yīng wú suǒ zhù ér shēng qí xīn
translation_en: Let the mind arise without fixing it anywhere.

original_transliteration: kǒu tóng tiě shuān wú ěr kǒng quē
translation_en: The Great Way has no gate; a thousand paths lead to it. Pass through this barrier and walk freely through the universe.
Everyday mind is the Way.
Vast emptiness, nothing holy. Bodhidharma's reply reveals that the sacred is not separate from the ordinary.
Just sitting: zazen is not a means to awakening, it is awakening itself.

original_transliteration: hotoke no dō o narau to iu wa, jiko o narau nari
translation_en: To study the Buddha Way is to study the self; to study the self is to forget the self.
Being-time: each moment of existence is all of time; time does not fly away.

original_transliteration: takigi wa hai to naru, sara ni kaeri te takigi to naru bekarazu
translation_en: Firewood becomes ash and does not become firewood again; yet do not suppose ash is after and firewood before.
There is a true person of no rank who is always going in and out through the gates of your face.

original_transliteration: féng fó shā fó féng zǔ shā zǔ
translation_en: If you meet the Buddha on the road, strike him down. Do not seek outside yourself.

original_transliteration: zhí zhǐ rén xīn jiàn xìng chéng fó
translation_en: Point directly at the human mind; see your true nature and become a Buddha.
Nine years facing a wall: stillness and perseverance are the foundation of awakening.

original_transliteration: yún zài qīng tiān shuǐ zài píng
translation_en: Clouds in the blue sky, water in the jug. Each thing rests perfectly in its own nature.
In the beginner's mind there are many possibilities; in the expert's mind there are few.
Equal respect for all paths of truth.
Non-violence everywhere, always, devoted to the welfare of all beings.
source: Shared across Hindu, Jain, Buddhist traditions

translation_en: As the seed, so the fruit; every action bears its own consequence.
source: Shared karma teaching across Dharmic traditions

translation_en: Compassion is the root of all spiritual life.
source: Shared across Buddhist, Hindu, Jain, Sikh traditions

translation_en: The company of the truthful transforms the seeker; seek holy fellowship.
source: Shared: Hindu satsang, Sikh sadh sangat, Buddhist sangha

translation_en: The grace of the teacher alone opens the door to liberation.
source: Shared across Hindu, Sikh, Zen traditions

translation_en: The divine light shines within every being; awaken the lamp of awareness in the heart.
source: Shared: Hindu antaryami, Sikh jot, Buddhist Buddha-nature

translation_en: Detachment from worldly entanglement is praised across all paths as the gateway to freedom.
source: Shared: Hindu vairagya, Buddhist nekkhamma, Jain aparigraha

translation_en: In silence the mind discovers its own nature and the voice of truth is heard.
source: Shared: Hindu mauna, Buddhist noble silence, Zen stillness

translation_en: Selfless service to others is the highest expression of spiritual life.
source: Shared: Sikh seva, Hindu paropakara, Buddhist dana

translation_en: All things are impermanent; understanding this deeply brings freedom from clinging.
source: Shared: Buddhist anicca, Hindu anitya, Jain kshana-bhangura

translation_en: Truth is the foundation of all dharma; speak truth, live truth, be truth.
See all beings with equal vision; the divine resides equally in all.
source: Shared: Gita samadarshana, Sikh ek jot, Buddhist metta for all

translation_en: Liberation is the ultimate aim: freedom from the cycle of suffering, known by many names across traditions.
source: Shared: Hindu moksha, Buddhist nibbana, Sikh mukti, Jain kevala

translation_en: Act with full awareness in every moment; mindful action transforms the mundane into the sacred.
source: Shared: Buddhist sati, Hindu smriti, Sikh surati, Zen mindfulness

translation_en: One who sees the Supreme Lord dwelling equally in all beings does not degrade the self by the self.
Non-violence, truth, non-stealing, continence, and non-possessiveness are the great universal vows.

original_transliteration: pavan gurū pānī pitā mātā dharat mahat
translation_en: Air is the Guru, water the father, and the great earth the mother of all.

original_transliteration: sabbe saṅkhārā dukkhā ti yadā paññāya passati
translation_en: When one sees with wisdom that all formations are suffering, one turns away from the path of sorrow.
Speak what is true and what is beneficial to all beings.

original_transliteration: pútí běn wú shù, míng jìng yì fēi tái
translation_en: Bodhi originally has no tree; the bright mirror has no stand. There is nothing from the start; where can dust alight?
